calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-22-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-24-2020 Continued To Feb-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant City And County Of San Francisco
Off Calendar Jul-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Ryan Stevens for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted.

Plaintiff Bobby James alleges he "tripped on a hole where a [parking] meter used to be" while on a 3rd Street sidewalk in October 2015. (Cmplt. p. 4.)

To prevail on his dangerous condition of public property claim, James must prove that the City either created the condition or had actual or constructive notice of it in sufficient time to take protective measures. (Gov. Code 835.) No evidence indicates that anything the City did - as opposed to, e.g., a traffic accident - created the condition. Further, the City's 311 database shows no reports of it. (Dyer Dec.) And James' testimony that he did not see a hole when walking past the same spot five days earlier suggests it was of short duration and thus would not have been noted by periodic inspections. (James Dep. 49-50.)

The summary judgment "papers submitted show there is no triable issue as to any material fact and the moving party is entitled to judgment as a matter of law." (CCP 437c(c).)

order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-29-2019 Continued To Sep-10-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION - Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Case As Unlimited Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Feb-26-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Lee, Esq. 415-398-5000, the Law Offices of Brian L. Larsen for Plaintiff.
Kelly Collins, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800, Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CCSF.
James Picker, Esq. 925-225-6838 of Philip M. Andersen & Associates through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF BOBBY JAMES' Motion To Reclassify Case As Unlimited Jurisdiction.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Bobby James' motion to reclassify this action as an unlimited jurisdiction proceeding is denied. This personal injury action arising out of an accident that occurred on October 18, 2015 was filed on August 2, 2016, and all of the evidence of Plaintiff's medical treatment dates to 2015 and 2016. At Plaintiff's deposition, which was taken on December 15, 2016, Plaintiff testified that he was suffering residual pain from the accident. Plaintiff has failed to show good cause for not seeking reclassification earlier. (Code Civ. Proc. 403.040(b)(2).) That counsel delayed for more than two years until shortly before the rescheduled arbitration in January 2019 before undertaking to fully evaluate the case, despite the earlier availability of medical records and other information that would have enabled such an evaluation, does not constitute such good cause. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION - Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Case As Unlimited Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Feb-11-2019 Continued To Feb-26-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BOBBY JAMES' Motion To Reclassify Case As Unlimited Jurisdiction.

Plaintiff Bobby Jones' motion to reclassify case as unlimited jurisdiction is continued to February 26, 2019. The court exercises it discretion per Jay v. Mahaffery (2013) 218 Cal. App. 4th 1522 to consider the evidence submitted with the reply papers and affords defendant City and County of San Francisco the opportunity to file supplemental opposition papers no later than February 19, 2019.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
